metadata.description: 索引类型: Different index types are suitable for various query patterns. To illustrate with a simple Python code example using different indexing methods in databases (considering `pandas` DataFrame as an example): ```python import pandas as pd # Sample data data = {'Name': ['John', 'Anna', 'Peter', 'Linda'], 'Age': [28, 22, 35, 40], 'City': ['New York', 'Paris', 'Berlin', 'London']} df = pd.DataFrame(data) # Creating a DataFrame with an index df.set_index('Name', inplace=True) print("DataFrame with Index set to 'Name':") print(df) # Using loc for label-based indexing print("\nAccessing data using .loc:") print(df.loc['Anna']) # Using iloc for integer position based indexing print("\nAccessing data using .iloc:") print(df.iloc[1]) # Reverting back to default index df.reset_index(inplace=True) ``` This example demonstrates how different types of indexing (`set_index`, `loc`, and `iloc`) can be used in a DataFrame, which is analogous to choosing the right type of index for database queries.
